title: HTTP Response Code Trending
spl:
index=security tag=web
| timechart span=5m count(eval(status>=500)) as s5 count(eval(status>=400 AND status<500)) as s4
description: 5xx and 4xx rates from CIM `status` for SLO dashboards.
value: 5xx and 4xx rates from CIM `status` for SLO dashboards.
implementation: Split by `app` or `url` top paths; alert on s5 >1% of traffic.
cimSpl: | tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.status>=400 by Web.status, _time span=5m
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Web CIM.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `status`, `url`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Split by `app` or `url` top paths; alert on s5 >1% of traffic.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=web
| timechart span=5m count(eval(status>=500)) as s5 count(eval(status>=400 AND status<500)) as s4
```

Understanding this SPL

**HTTP Response Code Trending** — 5xx and 4xx rates from CIM `status` for SLO dashboards.

Documented **Data sources**: `status`, `url`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Web CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=5m** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.status>=400 by Web.status, _time span=5m
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**HTTP Response Code Trending** — 5xx and 4xx rates from CIM `status` for SLO dashboards.

Documented **Data sources**: `status`, `url`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Web CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Web.Web` — enable acceleration for that model.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (5xx/4xx), Single value (error rate).
grandmaExplanation: 5xx and 4xx rates from for service goals dashboards — so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
